events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need produce JSON answer. Need decide compliance. Scenario: manufacturing/assembly floor, hot work, no fire watch during + post-work period. Work rough-complete. NFPA 51B and OSHA 1926 Subpart J require fire watch during hot work and for a sufficient period after (e.g., 30 minutes or more) to detect smoldering fires. No fire watch = non-compliant, dangerous. Decision fail. Risk high or critical? Hot work without fire watch in production floor could lead to fire; likely high. Component conditions: installed-defective? non-compliant? The work itself is non-compliant due to missing fire watch. Could label non-compliant. Maybe installed-defective? Since hot work roughed in but missing required fire watch, condition categories apply: non-compliant. Could also installed-defective? The component condition categories that may apply: installed-defective, non-compliant. Missing fire watch is a procedural non-compliance, not necessarily component defective. Use non-compliant. percent_complete maybe 70. value 0. confidence 0.95. event_time 0, rate 0, order empty, workable empty. findings: no fire watch during and after hot work; fire watch absent. actions: assign trained fire watch during hot work and maintain for required post-work period (e.g., 30+ min); inspect area; implement hot work permit. rationale: NFPA 51B and OSHA require fire watch. references: NFPA 51B, OSHA 1926 Subpart J. Need ensure JSON valid.

    "No fire watch is maintained for the required post-work period after hot work has ceased."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Halt hot work until a trained fire watch is assigned.",
    "Provide a dedicated fire watch during hot work and for the required post-work surveillance period (e.g., at least 30 minutes or per site policy).",
    "Inspect the work area and adjacent spaces for ignition, sparks, or smoldering material.",
    "Implement and enforce a hot work permit program per NFPA 51B and OSHA requirements."
  ],
  "rationale": "NFPA 51B and OSHA 1926 Subpart J require a fire watch during hot work and continued surveillance afterward to detect and extinguish smoldering fires. The observed absence of a fire watch makes the work non-compliant and creates a serious fire hazard.",
  "references": [
    "NFPA 51B",
    "OSHA 1926 Subpart J"
  ]
}
